title: Child Immunization Schedule Notes | Vaccines & Immunizations | CDC
text:

Monkeypox vaccination 
(minimum age: 18 years [Jynneos])
Special situations 
Age 18 years and at risk for monkeypox infection : complete 2-dose series, 28 days apart.
Risk factors for monkeypox infection include: 
Persons who are gay or bisexual and other MSM, transgender, or nonbinary people who in the past 6 months have had:
A new diagnosis of at least 1 sexually transmitted disease 
More than 1 sex partner 
Sex at a commercial sex venue 
Sex in association with a large public event in a geographic area where monkeypox virus transmission is occurring 
Persons who are sexual partners of the persons described above 
Persons who anticipate experiencing any of the situations described above 
Pregnancy: There is currently no ACIP recommendation for Jynneos use in pregnancy due to lack of safety data in pregnant women. Pregnant women with any risk factor described above may receive Jynneos 
For detailed information, see: www.cdc.gov/mpox/hcp/vaccine-considerations/vaccination-overview.html 
Contraindications and Precautions 
For contraindications and precautions to Mpox vaccination, see Mpox Appendix 
Pneumococcal vaccination 
(minimum age: 6 weeks [PCV15], [PCV 20]; 2 years [PPSV23])
Routine vaccination with PCV 
4-dose series at 2, 4, 6, 12–15 months 
Catch-up vaccination with PCV
